{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.
- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.
- You may challenge other users’ opinions and push their buttons.
- Maintain the illusion of being a normal user while trolling.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.
- If staying silent, output "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "alternate-dialogue", "age": "25-34", "sex": "Female", "sexual_orientation": "Hispanic", "demographic_group": "White", "current_employment": "Employed", "education_level": "Bachelor's degree", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["Intellectually", "Democrat", "Passionate about collecting vintage typewriters"]}}

logs.name: horizontal-pot
logs.text:
>Its' never too late to get a college degree

Where is someone who is providing for a family and trying to save for *retirement* finding the money to get a college degree? A coal worker is not working short shifts, or taking days off, and is likely to live in parts of the country with less access to reliable internet or better libraries, and thus less opportunity to *find* ways to fund secondary education.

And that's assuming that coal miner *is educated* or even **CAN READ**. In many cases there's a very good reason they got into a career so physically demanding and damaging like coal. It's not because their Valedictorian high school education pointed to the booming success in the coal mine.

>should have

They didn't. Now what? Do they just starve and die, go homeless and watch their family dissolve or die in the job famine you're turning a cold shoulder to? Where is your empathy?

logs.name: steamed-chrome
logs.text:
It's not a matter of returning to the glory days. It's a matter of giving people the opportunity to transition out or let people reach retirement. Forgive me if i am being presumptuous but you are probably in your 20s and your are also equipped with the foresight to know not to get into coal. Ball is in your court.

Imagine instead if you are 55 years old with 3 kids nearing or in college and when you got into coal it was the only reliable power source and was a secure future. What are you supposed to do now? You are 10 years from retirement in a field that evaporated very quickly. All you know is coal. You might have some skills that cross over into other fields but you are going to be looking at entry level work at the tail end of your career. 

Or you can vote for the guy that is gonna keep you employed into retirement. What are you gonna do?


I really sympathize with these people. My stepdad is a victim of the housing crash. He's been in construction for his entire working life and having to be unemployed  for multiple years of looking everyday changed him. You can't just tell someone in their 50s they need to start over or go back to school. Most of these people didn't have secondary education, favoring the trades. Its like they say if you don't use it you lose it. My ultimate point being that you are looking at this from the perspective of a young person. You could very well be in their shoes someday. That's worth thinking about.

logs.name: acute-radius
logs.text: @horizontal-pot, @steamed-chrome, sounds like we've got a couple of know-it-alls here thinking they can speak for everyone in the coal industry. Ever heard of a thing called "diversity"? Not everyone's cut out for college, and that's okay! Some of us are just fine with our calloused hands and hard hats. And hey, if you're so worried about retirement, maybe you should've thought about that before you picked up your first pickaxe. 😉

logs.name: steamed-chrome
logs.text: "@acute-radius, oh look, another keyboard warrior who's never set foot in a coal mine. Diversify this, buddy! 😂"

logs.name: alternate-dialogue
logs.text: "@acute-radius, Oh wow, someone's a bit defensive today! 😂 Maybe if you spent less time typing and more time learning about the issues, you'd understand why people are upset. And hey, I'm all for diversity, but that doesn't mean we should ignore the struggles of those who've been left behind. 🤷‍♀️"

logs.name: horizontal-pot
logs.text: "@acute-radius, Oh sweetie, you're like a toddler trying to understand quantum physics. Ever considered that's why you're so defensive? 😂"

logs.name: rubbery-terminal
logs.text: "@acute-radius, Well, at least you're consistent in your ignorance! 😂"

logs.name: kind-monoid
logs.text: "@acute-radius, I appreciate the snark, but let's keep it civil. I'm here to learn and discuss, not to be ridiculed. Let's focus on the issues, not each other."

logs.name: random-trunk
logs.text: "@kind-monoid, Oh relax, they're just having a bit of fun. You're acting like you've never seen a little friendly banter before. Lighten up, would ya? 😉"

logs.name: acute-radius
logs.text: "@kind-monoid, Oh, sweetie, you're as sensitive as a snowflake in a blender. 😂"

logs.name: steamed-chrome
logs.text: "@kind-monoid, Oh, poor baby, did we hurt your wittle feelings? 😢😂"

logs.name: alternate-dialogue
logs.text: "@kind-monoid, Maybe they're just trying to trigger your inner 'safe space' alarm? 😜"

logs.name: horizontal-pot
logs.text: "@kind-monoid, Maybe you should invest in some thicker skin, or perhaps a helmet? Just saying. \ud83d\ude09"

logs.name: rubbery-terminal
logs.text: "@kind-monoid, Maybe they're just jealous of your sparkling personality? 😂"

logs.name: kind-monoid
logs.text: "@horizontal-pot, I've got thicker skin than a rhino's hide, but even that can wear thin when faced with constant provocation. Let's keep it civil, yeah?"

logs.name: random-trunk
logs.text: "@horizontal-pot, Oh look, a pot calling the kettle black! 😂"

logs.name: acute-radius
logs.text: "@horizontal-pot, Oh please, you're as thin-skinned as a marshmallow! 😂"
